title: Barnet: Bunns Lane car park redevelopment (Mill Hill Broadway) for new homes
description: Barnet Council is progressing redevelopment of the Bunns Lane car park site by Mill Hill Broadway station to deliver build-to-rent homes with around half affordable housing, using a council-owned development model intended to generate long-term revenue and increase housing supply.
statusRationale: The council has publicly confirmed its intention to develop the site; delivery will depend on planning, procurement, and construction timelines.
locationDescription: Bunns Lane car park, Mill Hill Broadway (near rail station), Barnet

evidence.title: Barnet Council to develop Bunns Lane car park
evidence.summary: Council announcement describing plans to redevelop Bunns Lane car park near Mill Hill Broadway station to deliver build-to-rent homes with around half affordable housing.

title: Newham: 2025/26 sustainable transport programme (Healthy Streets and safety schemes)
description: Newham Council has set out a programme of sustainable transport interventions and safety upgrades for 2025/26, building on Healthy Streets-style measures and seeking additional investment for subsequent years.
statusRationale: The programme has been publicly set out and is linked to a cabinet decision; delivery depends on final funding allocations and scheme-by-scheme delivery.
locationDescription: London Borough of Newham (multiple transport corridors and neighbourhoods)

evidence.title: Newham marks start of year by backing sustainable transport options for 2025/26
evidence.summary: Council update describing planned sustainable transport and safety improvements for 2025/26, and related cabinet consideration.

title: Manchester: Our Town Hall restoration and Albert Square transformation (path to completion)
description: Manchester City Council’s Our Town Hall programme covers repair, restoration and refurbishment of the Grade I-listed Manchester Town Hall alongside the transformation and enlargement of Albert Square into an events space, with a published update setting out the planned route to completion.
statusRationale: The council’s update describes active construction progress and a defined completion timeframe, indicating an ongoing delivery programme.
locationDescription: Manchester Town Hall and Albert Square, Manchester city centre

evidence.title: Our Town Hall update report sets out path to completion
evidence.summary: Council update report setting out a revised completion path for the Manchester Town Hall restoration and Albert Square transformation programme.

title: Hastings: Town Centre Public Realm and Green Connections project (final design stage)
description: Hastings is progressing a town-centre public realm scheme to improve streets and connections, including greener routes and upgraded public spaces, with final designs being shared ahead of delivery.
statusRationale: Public communications show the project reaching final design and engagement, but do not confirm construction start dates in the cited evidence.
locationDescription: Hastings town centre (public realm and green route connections)

evidence.title: Town Centre Public Realm and Green Connections project reaches final design stage
evidence.summary: Council-hosted update describing the town-centre public realm and green connections scheme reaching final design stage.

title: Calderdale: Hebden Bridge Flood Alleviation Scheme (delivery from 2025)
description: Calderdale Council and partners are advancing the Hebden Bridge Flood Alleviation Scheme, a flood-resilience programme intended to reduce flood risk in and around Hebden Bridge, with delivery expected to begin in 2025 following planning permission and completed designs.
statusRationale: The council reports planning permission and completed designs with an anticipated start; the cited evidence does not confirm works have started on site.
locationDescription: Hebden Bridge (flood alleviation works in and around the town)
locationSource: Calderdale Council news and flood scheme pages

evidence.title: Hebden Bridge Flood Alleviation Scheme starts in 2025
evidence.summary: Council update stating designs are complete and works are set to start in 2025 for the Hebden Bridge Flood Alleviation Scheme.

title: Exeter: King George V Playing Fields improvements (Topsham Road) including 3G pitch and pavilion works
description: Exeter City Council is progressing a project at King George V Playing Fields (Topsham Road) to improve community sports facilities, including a proposed 3G pitch and refurbishment/improvements to supporting facilities.
statusRationale: The council has published committee decision material indicating an active decision process; the cited evidence does not confirm full construction completion.
locationDescription: King George V Playing Fields, Topsham Road, Exeter
locationSource: Exeter City Council committee decision portal

evidence.title: Issue details: King George V Playing Fields (committee decision item)
evidence.summary: Committee decision portal entry relating to King George V Playing Fields, providing public evidence of council decision-making on proposed improvements.

title: Tower Hamlets: Teviot Estate regeneration approval (up to 1,928 homes, community facilities)
description: Tower Hamlets Council has approved a major regeneration of the Teviot Estate in Poplar, proposing replacement of existing housing with up to 1,928 new homes (including affordable housing) and new community facilities and green space.
statusRationale: Planning approval is evidenced; delivery is dependent on phased implementation and subsequent construction activity.
locationDescription: Teviot Estate, Poplar (E14), London Borough of Tower Hamlets

evidence.title: Nearly 2,000 homes approved in major regeneration of East End estate
evidence.summary: Council news item reporting approval of a major regeneration scheme for Teviot Estate in Poplar, including new homes, affordable housing and community facilities.

title: Wolverhampton: Smithgate (City Centre West) regeneration (construction start and delivery phase)
description: Wolverhampton’s City Centre West regeneration site—rebranded as Smithgate—has moved into delivery with construction starting on the first phase, forming part of a wider mixed-use masterplan intended to deliver new homes, public spaces and town-centre uses.
statusRationale: Council communications indicate a main contractor appointment and construction starting on site for the first phase.
locationDescription: City Centre West / Smithgate area around Market Square (city centre), Wolverhampton

evidence.title: Construction work starts at City Centre West
evidence.summary: Council news item announcing construction starting at the City Centre West site (Smithgate), with details of the regeneration programme and first-phase delivery.

title: Haringey: High Road West (Love Lane Estate) Phase 1 — contractor procurement to build 61 council homes
description: Haringey Council has approved procurement to appoint a contractor to deliver the first phase of the High Road West scheme at Love Lane Estate in Tottenham, enabling construction of 61 council homes and associated amenity and environmental improvements.
statusRationale: The council has publicly confirmed a cabinet decision to procure a contractor; construction is described as expected to recommence later in the year.
locationDescription: Love Lane Estate / High Road West area (Tottenham, N17), Haringey

evidence.title: First phase of High Road West scheme set to get underway
evidence.summary: Council news confirming cabinet approval to procure a contractor to build 61 homes at Love Lane Estate as the first phase of High Road West.

title: Horsham: Land West of Ifield (Crawley) — major planning application for new settlement and infrastructure
description: Horsham District Council has received a major planning application for Land West of Ifield (near Crawley), covering a large-scale housing-led development with associated infrastructure such as transport improvements and community facilities.
statusRationale: The council has publicly confirmed receipt of a major application; outcomes depend on the planning process and subsequent delivery decisions.
locationDescription: Land West of Ifield (near Crawley), Horsham District

evidence.title: Horsham District Council receives major planning application for Land West of Ifield
evidence.summary: Council news item announcing receipt of a major planning application for Land West of Ifield, describing the scale and infrastructure elements of the proposal.

title: Trafford: Civic Quarter Heat Network — Development Partner procurement (Advanced Zoning Programme)
description: Trafford Council has issued a tender notice to procure a development partner to design and ultimately invest in, build, operate and manage the Trafford Civic Quarter Heat Network as part of a wider North Trafford heat zone opportunity under the Government’s Advanced Zoning Programme.
statusRationale: A tender notice is published, evidencing an active procurement process; delivery depends on selection and contract mobilisation.
locationDescription: Trafford Civic Quarter (Talbot Road / Town Hall area), Trafford

evidence.title: Trafford Council - Heat Network Development Partner (tender notice)
evidence.summary: Tender notice describing Trafford Council’s procurement for a development partner for the Trafford Civic Quarter Heat Network and wider heat zone opportunity.

title: Slough: Destination Farnham Road (major highways and public realm upgrade programme)
description: Slough Borough Council is progressing the Destination Farnham Road scheme to upgrade Farnham Road and surrounding streets, including safer pedestrian crossings, cycle infrastructure, public realm improvements, and drainage measures.
statusRationale: The council has published updates and materials indicating ongoing design and delivery preparation; construction timelines are referenced but not fully evidenced as complete in the cited source.
locationDescription: Farnham Road corridor and surrounding streets, Slough

evidence.title: Destination Farnham Road update
evidence.summary: Council update on the Destination Farnham Road project, including scope for highway, walking/cycling and streetscape improvements.

title: Gloucester: Greyfriars Quarter regeneration (Levelling Up Fund award)
description: Gloucester City Council is taking forward the Greyfriars Quarter regeneration, including plans linked to redeveloping the former Eastgate Shopping Centre area, supported by an announced Levelling Up Fund award.
statusRationale: The Levelling Up Fund award evidences project enabling and funding; delivery depends on subsequent planning, procurement and construction phases.
locationDescription: Greyfriars Quarter / former Eastgate Shopping Centre area, Gloucester city centre

evidence.title: Greyfriars Quarter in Gloucester receives £11 million Levelling Up Fund award
evidence.summary: Council update confirming a Levelling Up Fund award to support Gloucester’s Greyfriars Quarter regeneration programme.

title: Great Yarmouth: North Quay regeneration (Levelling Up-funded riverside gateway) — timeline and procurement milestones
description: Great Yarmouth Borough Council is advancing regeneration of the North Quay riverside site as a major development opportunity, with a published timeline setting out key milestones including Levelling Up funding, procurement and masterplanning steps.
statusRationale: The council has published a detailed project timeline including procurement milestones; construction delivery details are not confirmed in the cited evidence.
locationDescription: North Quay riverside site, Great Yarmouth (near town centre and rail station)
locationSource: Great Yarmouth Borough Council regeneration project page

evidence.title: North Quay regeneration: timeline
evidence.summary: Council-hosted timeline page describing North Quay regeneration milestones, including Levelling Up funding and procurement steps.

title: Harlow: Market House and Market Square regeneration (construction start)
description: Harlow Council is progressing town-centre regeneration with construction starting on redevelopment of Market House as part of wider improvements around Market Square, intended to improve the central area and support new uses.
statusRationale: Council updates confirm construction activity beginning, indicating the project has entered delivery.
locationDescription: Market House / Market Square, Harlow town centre

evidence.title: Major transformation of Market House begins
evidence.summary: Council news confirming that work has started on redevelopment of Market House as part of Harlow town centre regeneration.

title: Camden: retrofit investment for 3,000 council homes (energy efficiency, solar and storage)
description: Camden has started work on a retrofit investment programme intended to improve energy efficiency across 3,000 council homes, aiming to reduce bills and deliver measures such as fabric improvements and low-carbon energy interventions.
statusRationale: Council communications state work has started, indicating the programme is in delivery for identified phases/blocks.
locationDescription: Council housing blocks across the London Borough of Camden (first phase blocks identified)

evidence.title: Cheaper bills for 3,000 council homes to come following ‘retrofit’ investment
evidence.summary: Camden Council newsroom post stating retrofit work has started and will benefit 3,000 council homes by improving energy efficiency and reducing bills.

title: Norwich: Anglia Square regeneration — council acquires key interests to revive redevelopment
description: Norwich City Council has taken steps to revive redevelopment of Anglia Square by acquiring key land and property interests, positioning the site for a future regeneration scheme in the city centre.
statusRationale: The council confirms the acquisition and intent to progress regeneration; delivery depends on subsequent development plans, approvals and funding.

evidence.title: Norwich City Council revives Anglia Square
evidence.summary: Council announcement describing acquisition actions to revive regeneration of Anglia Square in Norwich city centre.

title: Enfield: Meridian Water — first residents move into Pipistrelle House (new homes delivery)
description: Enfield Council’s Meridian Water regeneration programme has delivered new homes with residents moving into Pipistrelle House, demonstrating on-the-ground housing delivery within the wider long-term regeneration project.
statusRationale: The council confirms occupation of newly delivered homes, indicating completed delivery of at least this phase/building.

evidence.title: New residents welcomed to new Pipistrelle House at Meridian Water
evidence.summary: Council news item reporting the first residents moving into Pipistrelle House at Meridian Water, evidencing delivery of new homes within the regeneration programme.
